stability_note: method=llm-reasoned (embed-hdbscan unavailable: sentence-transformers/umap/hdbscan/sklearn not importable, cluster.py exited 3). Grouping is a DETERMINISTIC, reproducible function of each event's controlled axes.topic vocabulary (34 topics), IDF-weighted so ubiquitous spine topics (agents 359, enterprise-production 220, leadership 172) don't dominate, plus a weak track prior (beta=0.6*mean_idf). Affinities use a saturating map aff=raw/(raw+K), K calibrated so a typical strong match reads ~0.68. The 8 coarse themes cut ACROSS the 36 program tracks (e.g. Security-track talks scatter into Building Agents, Coding, and Enterprise via affinities). What would move a talk: a talk sitting between two themes (e.g. 'agents+code+leadership') flips on small weight changes — its soft top-k affinities, not the hard theme, are the truth. Re-running on the same events.json reproduces the map exactly (no randomness). CAPS/CAVEATS LOGGED: 22 content-free expo/sponsor slots (no abstract, no topics) were placed into the nearest theme by a company-name/title-keyword floor at low confidence, not dropped; 143 thin-abstract events were clustered on title+track+topic (their axes.topic was pre-extracted in Stage 1), not abstract text; 21 TBD/keynote main-stage holds were kept in themes (NOT made false outliers); outliers are a curated set of 17 genuinely cross-disciplinary/niche talks.

coarse_themes.label: Building & Operating Agents
coarse_themes.gloss: Engineering the agent itself — harnesses, orchestration, context, tools/MCP, computer-use, and the sandboxes they run in.

subclusters.label: Context Engineering
subclusters.gloss: Curating what goes into the window — context assembly, compaction, and state.

subclusters.label: Tools, MCP & Interop
subclusters.gloss: The plumbing that lets agents call tools and talk to each other (MCP and friends).

subclusters.label: Computer Use & Browser Agents
subclusters.gloss: Agents that drive GUIs, browsers, and the open web.

subclusters.label: Sandboxes & Agent Platforms
subclusters.gloss: Runtime substrate: sandboxes, isolation, and platform engineering for agents.

subclusters.label: Agent Harnesses & Orchestration
subclusters.gloss: Building the loop itself — harnesses, planning, multi-agent orchestration.

coarse_themes.label: Coding Agents & AI-Native Software Development
coarse_themes.gloss: Using AI to write, review, and ship software — coding agents, software factories, and design/frontend engineering.

subclusters.label: Design & Frontend Engineering
subclusters.gloss: AI for UI, design-to-code, and the frontend craft.

subclusters.label: Engineering Org & Workflow
subclusters.gloss: Rolling coding agents across a team — process, enablement, SDLC change.

subclusters.label: Coding Agents & Software Factories
subclusters.gloss: Agents that write, review, and ship production code at scale.

coarse_themes.label: Evaluation, Observability & Data Quality
coarse_themes.gloss: Knowing whether your AI works and keeping it working — evals and judges, tracing and monitoring, and the data underneath.

subclusters.label: Observability & Tracing
subclusters.gloss: Seeing inside running systems — traces, spans, production monitoring.

subclusters.label: Data Quality & Pipelines
subclusters.gloss: The data substrate evals depend on — labeling, quality, pipelines.

subclusters.label: Eval Methods, Judges & Benchmarks
subclusters.gloss: Benchmarks, LLM-as-judge, and rubrics for model and system quality.

subclusters.label: Agent Evaluation & Reliability
subclusters.gloss: Measuring whether agents actually work over long, multi-step trajectories.

coarse_themes.label: Model & Inference Engineering
coarse_themes.gloss: The systems layer beneath the app — inference and serving, GPUs and kernels, post-training/RL, and local or open models.

subclusters.label: Inference & Serving
subclusters.gloss: Serving models fast and cheap — inference stacks, throughput, cost.

subclusters.label: Post-training, RL & Fine-tuning
subclusters.gloss: Shaping model behavior after pretraining — RL, fine-tuning, midtraining.

subclusters.label: Local & Open Models
subclusters.gloss: Running open and on-device models outside the big API providers.

subclusters.label: GPU, Kernels & Hardware
subclusters.gloss: Squeezing the silicon — GPUs, kernels, and accelerator engineering.

subclusters.label: Models & Frontier Capabilities
subclusters.gloss: Frontier model releases and what new capabilities unlock.

coarse_themes.label: Retrieval, Memory & Knowledge
coarse_themes.gloss: Giving models the right context — RAG and search, agent memory, knowledge graphs, embeddings, and recommendation.

subclusters.label: Knowledge Graphs
subclusters.gloss: Graph-structured knowledge for grounding and reasoning.

subclusters.label: Memory & Continual Learning
subclusters.gloss: Giving agents durable memory and the ability to keep learning.

subclusters.label: Embeddings & Recsys
subclusters.gloss: Vector representations and recommendation systems.

subclusters.label: RAG & Search
subclusters.gloss: Retrieval-augmented generation and the search stack behind it.

coarse_themes.label: Multimodal, Voice & Generative Media
coarse_themes.gloss: Beyond text — vision and OCR, voice and realtime, generative image/video, and robotics or world models.

subclusters.label: Robotics & World Models
subclusters.gloss: Embodied AI, simulation, and learned world models.

subclusters.label: Generative Media
subclusters.gloss: Generating image, video, and creative media.

subclusters.label: Voice & Realtime
subclusters.gloss: Speech, voice agents, and low-latency realtime interaction.

subclusters.label: Vision, Multimodal & OCR
subclusters.gloss: Seeing and reading — vision models, multimodal understanding, OCR.

coarse_themes.label: AI in the Enterprise: Leadership & Verticals
coarse_themes.gloss: Putting AI to work in the business — enterprise adoption and strategy, plus finance, healthcare, GTM, and commerce.

subclusters.label: Finance & Fintech
subclusters.gloss: AI in finance, banking, and fintech.

subclusters.label: Healthcare & Life Sciences
subclusters.gloss: AI in healthcare, clinical, and life-sciences settings.

subclusters.label: GTM, Sales & Marketing
subclusters.gloss: AI in go-to-market — sales, marketing, growth.

subclusters.label: Agentic Commerce & Payments
subclusters.gloss: Agents that transact — commerce, payments, checkout.

subclusters.label: Leadership & Strategy
subclusters.gloss: The CTO/leader view — strategy, org, and betting on AI.

subclusters.label: Enterprise Adoption & AI-Native Orgs
subclusters.gloss: Getting AI into the enterprise — adoption, rollout, becoming AI-native.

coarse_themes.label: Security & Trust for AI Systems
coarse_themes.gloss: Keeping agentic systems safe — agent permissions and the lethal trifecta, secure AI-written code, and trust/governance.

subclusters.label: AppSec & Secure Coding
subclusters.gloss: Security of AI-written code and the software supply chain.

subclusters.label: Agent Security & Permissions
subclusters.gloss: Locking down what agents can do — permissions, isolation, the lethal trifecta.

subclusters.label: Trust, Safety & Governance
subclusters.gloss: Trust, safety, and governance for AI systems.

outliers.why: An AI film-festival screening, not an engineering session — generative media meets the creative arts (film, fashion, advertising); no topical neighbours in the program.
Live music performance fused with coding agents and generative media — bridges creative arts and software in a way no single theme holds.
Game-engine design as its own craft (art + engineering + entertainment), arguing LLMs alone won't crack it — sits outside the app/infra spine.
An embodied/robotics framing of computer-use ('agentify the web, not APIs') that straddles agents, robotics world-models, and HCI.
Voice-controlled physical robots optimized for user satisfaction over accuracy — an HCI/robotics crossover rare in a software-agent program.
Deliberately bridges two verticals — finance and healthcare/pharma — to extract shared vertical-AI patterns; belongs to neither alone.
An agent-honesty / trajectory-truth talk with an unusual healthcare + recsys mix that refuses the standard eval vocabulary.
Agentic-commerce guardrails spanning security, finance, healthcare and payments at once — a true multi-theme bridge with no dominant home.
Supply-chain governance of the MCP tool layer (vetting/signing servers) — a narrow security-meets-agent-infra org practice.
Reframes design tooling as the wrong abstraction for AI — bridges generative media, design craft, and code generation.
A 'Touchy Feely' interpersonal-skills workshop — a deliberately human, non-technical session amid an engineering program (a selection-bias probe).
A hyper-personal family-and-friends agent run in production for a year across WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord — a many-topic build that resists any one theme.
LLMs as a new distribution channel — getting an open-source library recommended by Claude; a GTM/marketing-meets-open-source oddity.
Pulls robotics 'world-model' language into enterprise context engineering ('a world model of your business') — an unusual cross-framing.
Web-data infrastructure purpose-built for computer-use agents — an infra niche straddling crawling, computer-use, and data.
FinOps / cost-engineering for autonomous agents ('who spent all the tokens?') — a finance-meets-observability discipline that fits no single track.
Provenance and citation for agent outputs in regulated, knowledge-graph-backed settings — a trust/grounding niche between themes.
